name: State Farm
description: The largest property and casualty insurance provider in the U.S., known for its extensive agent network.

name: Progressive
description: A leader in telematics and digital-first insurance, and a top auto insurer in the U.S.

name: GEICO
description: A subsidiary of Berkshire Hathaway, famous for its direct-to-consumer model and the Geico Gecko.

name: UnitedHealth Group
description: The world's largest healthcare company by revenue, offering diversified health and well-being services.

name: Liberty Mutual
description: A global insurer providing a wide range of property and casualty, health, and life insurance products.

name: MetLife
description: A global provider of life insurance, annuities, employee benefits, and asset management.

name: Aetna (CVS Health)
description: A major health insurer integrated with CVS Health's pharmacy and clinic network.

name: Travelers
description: One of the oldest U.S. insurers, a component of the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

name: Allstate
description: A major American insurer known for its 'Good Hands' slogan and broad range of personal products.

name: Prudential Financial
description: A leader in life insurance and investment management with the iconic Rock of Gibraltar logo.

name: Humana
description: Specializes in Medicare Advantage and senior-focused health insurance plans.

name: Nationwide
description: A large mutual insurance and financial services group based in the U.S.

name: The Hartford
description: A Fortune 500 company known for investment and insurance services, particularly for small businesses.

name: Cigna
description: Global health service company providing insurance and related products through employers.

name: Elevance Health (Anthem)
description: Formerly Anthem, this is the largest for-profit managed health care company in the Blue Cross Blue Shield Association.

name: USAA
description: Provides insurance, banking, and investment services exclusively to military members and their families.

name: Farmers Insurance
description: A group of companies providing home, auto, and life insurance, as well as financial services.

name: American International Group (AIG)
description: A major global insurance corporation with extensive U.S. commercial operations.

name: Kaiser Permanente
description: A unique non-profit healthcare organization that acts as both an insurer and a medical provider.

name: Lincoln Financial Group
description: Fortune 500 company providing life insurance, annuities, and retirement plan services.

name: MassMutual
description: A major mutual life insurance company serving millions of clients with life, retirement, and investment products.

name: New York Life
description: The largest mutual life-insurance company in the United States.

name: Centene
description: A multi-line healthcare enterprise that is the largest Medicaid managed care provider in the U.S.

name: Molina Healthcare
description: Focuses exclusively on government-sponsored healthcare programs including Medicaid and Medicare.

name: Erie Insurance
description: A high-ranking U.S. regional insurer known for customer service and stability.

name: Auto-Owners Insurance
description: A mutual insurance company consistently rated high for claims handling.

name: CNA Financial
description: One of the largest U.S. commercial property and casualty insurance companies.

name: American Family Insurance
description: A private mutual company that focuses on property, casualty, and auto insurance.

name: Guardian Life
description: One of the largest mutual life insurance companies in the U.S.

name: Principal Financial Group
description: A global financial investment management and insurance company based in the U.S.

name: Unum
description: The market leader in disability insurance and a major provider of financial protection benefits.

name: Hanover Insurance
description: Provides a wide range of property and casualty products through independent agents.

name: Markel
description: Often called a 'mini-Berkshire,' focusing on specialty insurance and diversified investments.

name: Pacific Life
description: Provides a wide range of life insurance products, annuities, and mutual funds.

name: Northwestern Mutual
description: A U.S. financial services mutual organization providing life insurance, financial planning, and wealth management.

name: TIAA
description: A leading provider of financial services in the academic, research, medical, cultural and governmental fields.

name: Aflac
description: The largest provider of supplemental insurance in the United States, famous for the Aflac Duck.

name: Mutual of Omaha
description: A Fortune 500 mutual insurance and financial services company.

name: Globe Life
description: Provides life and supplemental health insurance primarily to lower-middle to middle-income families.

name: Cincinnati Financial
description: A property casualty insurance group heavily reliant on independent agents for distribution.

name: W. R. Berkley
description: A Fortune 500 commercial lines property casualty insurance holding company.

name: Amica Mutual Insurance
description: The oldest mutual insurer of automobiles in the U.S., consistently highly rated for customer satisfaction.

name: Jackson Financial
description: A leading provider of annuities for retail investors in the United States.

name: Voya Financial
description: Helps Americans plan, invest, and protect their savings; focused heavily on retirement planning.

name: Thrivent
description: A not-for-profit financial services organization serving Christians with insurance and investments.

name: Securian Financial
description: Provides insurance, investment, and retirement solutions to individuals and businesses.

name: Sentry Insurance
description: A mutual insurance company offering business insurance, life insurance, and annuities.

name: Western & Southern Financial Group
description: A family of financial services companies focusing on life insurance and investment products.

name: CNO Financial Group
description: Provides life and health insurance, annuities, and financial services to middle-income Americans.

name: Symetra
description: Provides employee benefits, annuities, and life insurance products through a national network.
